key_insight: A time-aware tabular deep learning model can accurately predict inter-stop trolleybus energy use while also quantifying which operational factors causally drive consumption.
engineering_relevance: The method can support route planning, driving strategy, battery–catenary operation, and infrastructure decisions for dual-source trolleybus fleets.
method: Periodic time encoding + parameter-efficient batch-ensemble backbone for static and sequential inputs
Bayesian hyperparameter tuning with tree-structured density estimation
Three-stage causal analysis: feature attribution, LiNGAM causal discovery, and meta-learning for treatment effects
evidence_signal: On the Zurich trolleybus dataset with meteorological data, the model achieved 6.52% MAPE and 0.982 correlation, outperforming ten baselines.
watch_item: Causal driver estimates may depend on dataset quality, confounding control, and the validity of assumptions such as linear non-Gaussian acyclic structure.
paper_title: Inter-Stop Energy Prediction and Causal Driver Quantification for Dual-Source Trolleybuses via a Time-Aware Tabular Deep Learning Architecture
